title: Add ARIA labels, roles, and keyboard accessibility improvements
description: Add proper ARIA attributes (aria-label, role, aria-expanded, aria-live) to all interactive elements in the panel. Improve keyboard navigation with proper tab order, focus management, and visible focus indicators. Ensure all interactive elements meet the 44x44px touch target minimum. This addresses the accessibility gaps identified in exploration-patterns.json.

criteria: All icon-only buttons have aria-label attributes with descriptive text
Search results list has role='listbox' and individual results have role='option'
Component bar buttons have role='button' and aria-label with component name
Settings modal has role='dialog' and aria-modal='true'
Search input has aria-label and aria-autocomplete='list'
All interactive elements have minimum 44x44px click/touch target (padding if needed)
Focus indicators visible on all interactive elements (2px outline with offset)
Tab order follows visual layout: search input -> results -> component bar -> settings
aria-live='polite' on search results container for screen reader announcements
convergence.verification: Tab through all panel elements to verify focus order and visibility. Inspect DOM for ARIA attributes on every interactive element.

files.change: Add role, aria-label, aria-expanded, aria-modal, aria-live, aria-autocomplete attributes to interactive elements in the HTML template string

files.target: style property (CSS focus and target sizing)
files.change: Enhance focus-visible styles with consistent 2px outline + 2px offset; add minimum touch target sizing (44x44px) to buttons and clickable elements

files.target: JS methods generating dynamic HTML (onInput2, refreshCurComs, renderSettingsContent)
files.change: Add ARIA attributes to dynamically generated DOM elements (search results, component buttons, settings controls)
Add ARIA label translation keys for accessibility text (e.g., ARIA_SEARCH_INPUT, ARIA_SETTINGS_BUTTON, ARIA_FAVORITE_TOGGLE)

implementation: Audit all interactive elements in template HTML: buttons, inputs, links, clickable divs - catalog which ones lack ARIA attributes
Add role attributes: role='listbox' on #list, role='option' on search result items, role='dialog' on settings modal, role='toolbar' on component bar
Add aria-label to all icon-only buttons using i18n t() function for localized labels
Add aria-live='polite' to search results container (#list) so screen readers announce result count changes
Add aria-autocomplete='list' and aria-expanded to search input
Update dynamically generated HTML in JS methods (onInput2 result rendering, refreshCurComs component buttons) to include ARIA attributes
Enhance CSS focus-visible styles: consistent 2px solid outline with 2px offset on all interactive elements, using --primary-color for focus ring color
Add minimum sizing to ensure 44x44px touch targets on all buttons (add padding where needed)
Add corresponding i18n keys in i18n/zh.js for all new ARIA label strings
Verify tab order by testing keyboard-only navigation through the entire panel

reference.pattern: Existing prefers-reduced-motion and prefers-contrast media queries in CSS
reference.examples: ui-ux-pro-max rules: color-contrast 4.5:1, focus-states visible, aria-labels on icon buttons, touch-target-size 44x44px, keyboard-nav tab order matches visual order

rationale.chosen_approach: Add ARIA attributes directly to HTML template and dynamic HTML generation methods, with i18n support for label text
rationale.tradeoffs: Increases HTML template verbosity but ensures accessible experience for all users

manual_checks: Tab through entire panel - every interactive element receives focus with visible indicator
Screen reader (VoiceOver) announces all buttons, inputs, and dynamic content changes
All buttons meet 44x44px minimum touch target size
Settings dialog traps focus when open
success_metrics: 100% of interactive elements have aria-label or visible text label
Focus indicator visible on every focusable element
